title: Passive axis system for robotic surgical systems
abstract: A robotic surgical system has a user interface with a control arm that includes a passive axis system for maintaining degrees-of-freedom of a gimbal rotatably supported on the control arm as the gimbal is manipulated during a surgical procedure. The control arm includes a swivel member, a first member, and a second member. The swivel member is rotatable about a first axis. The first member rotatably coupled to the swivel member about a second axis that is orthogonal to the first axis. The second member rotatably coupled to the first member about a third axis that is parallel to the second axis. The gimbal rotatably supported by the second member about a fourth axis that is orthogonal to the third axis. The passive axis system correlating rotation of the swivel member about the first axis with rotation of the gimbal about the fourth axis.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED: 1. A control arm for receiving input from a user, the control arm comprising: a swivel member rotatable about a first axis in a first base direction to a first rotational limit and in a second base direction opposite the first direction to a second rotational limit; a first member pivotally coupled to the swivel member; a second member pivotally coupled to the first member; a gimbal rotatably supported about a fourth axis by the second member, rotation of the gimbal about the fourth axis operably associated with rotation of the swivel member about the first axis such that rotation of the gimbal in a first gimbal direction rotates the swivel member relative to the base about the first axis in the first base direction; and a limit extending mechanism configured to allow the gimbal to rotate about the fourth axis in the first gimbal direction when the swivel member is at the first rotational limit when an input torque in the first gimbal direction exceeds a threshold torque of the limit extending mechanism about the first axis. 2. The control arm according to claim 1, wherein the threshold torque is in a range of about 200 Nmm to about 1200 Nmm. 3. The control arm according to claim 1, wherein rotation of the gimbal in a second gimbal direction opposite the first gimbal direction rotates the swivel member relative to the base about the first axis in the second base direction. 4. The control arm according to claim 1, wherein the limit extending mechanism includes a plunger supporting a cam follower and a cam, the cam having a camming surface and the plunger biasing the cam follower into engagement with the camming surface of the cam. 5. The control arm according to claim 4, wherein the camming surface of the cam includes a well, the cam having a centered position when the cam follower is positioned within the well of the cam. 6. The control arm according to claim 5, wherein the cam is biased to the centered position by the engagement of the cam follower with the camming surface of the cam. 7. The control arm according to claim 5, wherein the threshold torque is equal to a torque required to rotate the cam from the centered position. 8. The control arm according to claim 7, wherein a cam profile of the camming surface, from the well to a midpoint of the camming surface, is shaped such that a constant input torque equal to the threshold torque is required for continued rotation of the cam to traverse the camming surface with the cam follower from the well towards the midpoint. 9. The control arm according to claim 7, wherein a cam profile of the camming surface, from the well to a midpoint of the camming surface, is shaped such that an 

 increasing input force is required for continued rotation of the cam to traverse the camming surface with the cam follower from the well towards the midpoint. 10. The control arm according to claim 9, wherein the radius of the cam profile increases such that a linearly increasing input torque is required for continued rotation of the cam. 11. The control arm according to claim 9, wherein the radius of the cam profile increases such that an exponentially increasing input torque is required for continued rotation of the cam. 12. The control arm according to claim 1, wherein the limit extending mechanism includes a motor having a drive shaft, a first pulley, a second pulley, and a drive belt, the first pulley rotatably fixed to the drive shaft of the motor, the second pulley rotatably fixed about the first axis and operably associated with the fourth axis, the drive belt disposed about the first and second pulleys. 13. The control arm according to claim 12, wherein the motor prevents rotation of the drive shaft when the input force is less than the threshold force. 14. A method of controlling a tool of a robotic system with a user interface, the method comprising: moving a gimbal of the user interface such that a control arm supporting the gimbal is rotated about a first axis in a first direction until the gimbal reaches a threshold 

 limit, the control arm including a limit exceeding mechanism that resists rotation of the gimbal in the first direction beyond the threshold limit; and applying an input torque to the gimbal in the first direction that is greater than a threshold torque of the limit exceeding mechanism such that the gimbal is rotated beyond the threshold limit. 15. The method according to claim 14, further comprising releasing the gimbal after gimbal is rotated beyond the threshold limit such that the limit exceeding mechanism returns the gimbal to the threshold limit. 16. The method according to claim 14, wherein the control arm is rotatably fixed about the first axis as the gimbal is rotated beyond the threshold limit.
